subject: Project Management
title: The Product Backlog
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Product Backlog.

hook.text: The product backlog is the single, ordered list of everything the product could ever become.

buildup.text: The Product Owner maintains it — adding, removing, and reordering items constantly.

discovery.text: Top items are detailed and ready. Bottom items are rough ideas. The backlog is a living document.

twist.text: A backlog that never shrinks is a symptom: the team is building faster than it's deciding.

climax.text: The best backlogs are short. Saying 'not now' is as important as saying 'yes.'

punchline.text: The list of what you won't build matters most.

quiz.question: Who is responsible for maintaining the Product Backlog?
